name: Zhuang
notes: Zhuang (Vahcuengh) — Tai language cluster of Guangxi, China; the spec targets Standard Zhuang, based on the Wuming dialect. INPUT CONTRACT: the official 1982 Latin orthography is NOT a plain letter→phoneme alphabet — the word-final letters ⟨z j x q h⟩ (and bare/unmarked) are TONE letters, not consonants, and the same letters are ordinary consonants elsewhere in the word. THE GRAPHEME INVENTORY IS NOT YET SPECIFIED here (graphemes/allophones are empty) because a correct map requires positional rules separating the tone-letter slot from the segmental slots, which needs sourcing before it can be claimed. The traditional Sawndip script is logographic (Han-derived characters) and no character map is claimed either.
